currentTask.name: Add normal-language workflow router
currentTask.goal: Let users type serious tasks normally while the workflow deterministically chooses the right lane, maps context when useful, and returns one next action

environment.note: This workspace runs entirely on Debian/WSL2. No PowerShell needed.
environment.primaryTerminal: WSL Debian with native Linux tools

whatChanged: Added workflow-router.sh as the hidden normal-language request router
Added /route command and propagation templates for OpenCode command discovery
Updated task-intake.sh so obvious tiny tasks route direct instead of research
Updated task-intake.sh so long-horizon knowledge-building goals route through shape-product and task-tree
Updated AGENTS, README, hub quickstart, and workspace overview to make /route the default internal path
Updated authoritative agent best practices with source-backed SWE-agent, Agentless, SWE-Dev, and OpenAI workflow lessons
Propagated route.md and workflow-router.sh to topic folders
verification: bash ./scripts/workflow-router.sh "I want to recreate Elemental Battlegrounds exactly for nostalgia" --limit 8
bash ./scripts/workflow-router.sh "I want to build a theory of reality from fundamental rules" --limit 6
residualRisk: The router is deterministic and useful, but it still relies on the orchestrator to continue the selected next action rather than merely reciting it.
immediateNextSteps: Commit the workflow-router changes in ai-prompting
Use normal-language prompts in topic folders and let /route choose the lane
If wrong-lane corrections recur, add examples to task-intake heuristics
Later consider a lightweight eval harness of representative prompts to test routing changes
resumeRules: Read session-state.json first on every resume
